column1: The User Friendly Budget Database is a summary database of data entered in the User Friendly Budgets prepared by New Jersey municipalities for their adopted budgets since 2015. Data on land area, population and aggregate property valuation were added from Census Bureau Population Estimates (2015-2019, 2021-2024), the 2020 Decennial Census (Redistricting Data), and DLGS Abstract of Ratables (2015-2024) data releases to put some of the data in context. The database contains data for each municipality that submitted a User Friendly Budget to the Division of Local Government Services. It does not include all data entered in User Friendly Budgets, but includes those major data points that cannot be found in other DLGS data releases. It is intended to make budget, staffing, personnel cost, debt, and financial information for New Jersey municipalities accessible in a flexible, easy-to-use format.

All data are self-reported by municipalities and have not been independently audited or verified. Municipalities that did not submit budgets in a given year or that submitted budget documents that could not be opened or analyzed will have no data for that year. Areas where municipalities entered no data or made obvious errors are marked with a dash (--) or a "no data" tag. The Department of Community Affairs is not responsible for the accuracy of this data. Questions about specific data points should be directed to the Chief Financial Officer of the municipality or municipalities concerned.
